sentences: Texas A&M Aggies (variously A&M or Texas Aggies) refers to the students, graduates, and sports teams of Texas A&M University.
The nickname "Aggie" was once common at land-grant or "Ag" (agriculture) schools in many states.
The teams compete in Division I of NCAA sports.
Texas A&M was a charter member of the Southwest Conference until its dissolution and subsequent formation of the Big 12 Conference in 1996.
The Aggies competed in the Big 12 until joining the Southeastern Conference (SEC) on July 1, 2012.
Texas A&M's official school colors are maroon and white.
The teams are referred to as Aggies and the mascot is a Rough Collie named Reveille.
The 2016 Big 12 Conference Baseball Tournament was held from May 25 through May 29 at Chickasaw Bricktown Ballpark in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma.
The annual tournament determined the conference champion of the Division I Big 12 Conference for college baseball.
The TCU Horned Frogs won the Tournament Championship, and as the winner of the tournament, TCU earned the league's automatic bid to the 2016 NCAA Division I Baseball Tournament.
The Big 12 Conference Baseball Tournament (sometimes known simply as the Big 12 Tournament) is the conference championship tournament in baseball for the Big 12 Conference.
Going back to the original format abandoned in 2005, the tournament consists of two separate four-team double-elimination tournaments.
The winners of each of those tournament face each other in a one-game match for the championship.
The winner receives the conference's automatic bid to the NCAA Division I Baseball Tournament.
The 2007 Big 12 Conference Baseball Tournament was held at AT&T Bricktown Ballpark in Oklahoma City, OK from May 23 through May 27.
Texas A&M won the tournament and earned the Big 12 Conference's automatic bid to the 2007 NCAA Division I Baseball Tournament.
This was the second year the conference used the round robin tournament setup.
The winners of each group at the end of the round robin faced each other in a one-game match for the championship.
The 1997 Big 12 Conference Baseball Tournament was the first in Big 12 history, and the only one to be held at All Sports Stadium in Oklahoma City, OK from May 15th through May 18th.
Oklahoma won the inaugural tournament and earned the Big 12 Conference's automatic bid to the 1997 NCAA Division I Baseball Tournament.
The format followed that used by the NCAA Division I Baseball Championship at the time: a six-team, double-elimination tournament.
The 1998 Big 12 Conference Baseball Tournament was the second played in Big 12 history, and the first to be held at AT&T Bricktown Ballpark in Oklahoma City, OK from May 14 through May 17.
Texas Tech won the tournament and earned the Big 12 Conference's automatic bid to the 1998 NCAA Division I Baseball Tournament.
The format followed that used by the NCAA Division I Baseball Championship at the time: a six-team, double-elimination tournament.
The 2007 Big East Conference Baseball Tournament was held at KeySpan Park in Brooklyn, NY.
This was the twenty third annual Big East Conference Baseball Tournament.
The won their third tournament championship and claimed the Big East Conference's automatic bid to the 2007 NCAA Division I Baseball Tournament.
The 2015 Big 12 Conference Baseball Tournament was held from May 20 through May 24 at ONEOK Field in Tulsa, Oklahoma.
The annual tournament determines the conference champion of the Division I Big 12 Conference for college baseball.
The winner of the tournament will earn the league's automatic bid to the 2015 NCAA Division I Baseball Tournament.
The 2017 Big 12 Conference Baseball Tournament will be held from May 24 through May 28 at Chickasaw Bricktown Ballpark in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma.
The annual tournament determines the conference champion of the Division I Big 12 Conference for college baseball.
The winner of the tournament will earn the league's automatic bid to the 2017 NCAA Division I Baseball Tournament.
The 1996 Big East Conference Baseball Tournament was held at Senator Thomas J. Dodd Memorial Stadium in Norwich, CT.
This was the twelfth annual Big East Conference Baseball Tournament.
The won their only tournament championship in their first year in the league, and claimed the Big East Conference's automatic bid to the 1996 NCAA Division I Baseball Tournament.
West Virginia joined the Big 12 Conference after the 2012 season.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Produce a single, concise, structured first-hop evidence report as the `summary`. The summary must do all of the following and only include content that helps determine what to fetch next:

- Enumerate concrete facts found in the provided passages. For each fact include the passage source label and either a very short quoted fragment (up to about 15 words) when a decisive short fact appears or a brief paraphrase otherwise. Keep each fact to one short line.

- Explicitly identify the bridge entity(ies) and any missing or ambiguous fact(s) that prevent answering the question from the given passages. If a name or identifier in the question corresponds to multiple candidate pages present in `passages`, list every candidate variant and mark the name as ambiguous; do not choose between them.

- Prescribe concrete, prioritized next-hop retrieval targets to resolve each missing or ambiguous element. For each target, specify which page(s) or disambiguation page to fetch and what exact section, keyword, or datum to extract (for example: specific field such as birth year, office held, list membership, dates, or the section heading to check). Prioritize targets (1, 2, 3...) when helpful.

- Only produce a final answer in `summary` if every required fact for every entity is present and unambiguous in the supplied passages; otherwise do not produce a final answer and focus on retrieval guidance.

Formatting and content constraints:
- Keep the summary concise (a short paragraph or a few short bullet-like lines).
- Produce only the single output field `summary` and nothing else.
- Preserve source-linked evidence; do not assert facts without a passage label.
- Do not invent facts or assume which ambiguous candidate is intended.
- Avoid copying long verbatim spans; use short quoted fragments when decisive and paraphrase otherwise.
- When an expected authoritative page is missing from `passages`, note that absence and include that page title as a next-hop retrieval target.

Input: fields `question` and `passages`. Output: one field `summary` meeting the requirements above.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

You are to produce exactly one compact second-hop retrieval query that targets the missing bridge or supporting evidence referenced by summary_1. Follow these operational rules when constructing the single `query` string:

- Use visible anchor tokens from `question` or `summary_1` (named works, titles, artist names, years or other identifiers only if present) and append a short retrieval cue naming the bridge sought (e.g., artist, album, recording, genre, season count, passage, supporting quote).
- Emit one concise line (one string assigned to `query`), roughly ≤ 10–12 words, formatted either as a short interrogative that names the anchors or as a compact keyword string (anchors + bridge keywords).
- If summary_1 explicitly requests a missing attribute or passage, emit the focused retrieval query rather than asking for clarification.
- Do not include answers, conclusions, enumerations, provenance notes, speculative language, or user-facing explanations; do not invent entities or add tokens that are not present in the inputs.
- Include year or other identifier tokens only if they appear in the provided inputs and help disambiguate.
- Keep wording retrieval-oriented and terse. Preserve the fallback that if retrieval using this query later fails, downstream steps may request clarification.

Output contract: produce exactly one field `query` whose value is the single compact retrieval query line described above.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single field only):
1. Merge the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into one compact integrated `summary` that preserves the primary answer name(s) and only includes passage-supported facts needed to justify or disambiguate that answer.
2. If `context` already contains a concise answer (a short sentence or a name), keep that content as the first sentence of `summary` when it is consistent with facts in `passages`. If `context` lacks a concise answer, create a concise first sentence that names the primary answer(s), using the canonical labels from `context` when available and otherwise from `passages`.
3. Expand the summary only when `passages` provide explicit, matching second-hop evidence about one or more named entities referenced in `context`. When expansion is appropriate, append at most one short supporting sentence per named entity. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's full name as shown in the inputs and paraphrase up to a few core attributes that are directly present in the `passages` (examples: nationality, a notable award/status, a current affiliation/team, a birth date, membership/partnership relation, or an explicit founding/formation fact).
4. Keep `summary` concise: typically 1–2 sentences for a single entity; allow up to 3 sentences only when multiple distinct named entities each require one supporting sentence. Prioritize the facts most directly necessary to answer the `question`.
5. Only assert facts that are directly supported by the provided `passages` or `context`. Do not introduce new entities, dates, awards, affiliations, roles, or causal claims that are not present in the inputs.
6. Prefer paraphrase over long verbatim copying. Use short quoted phrases only when the exact short phrase (roughly ≤10 words) appears in the `passages` and quoting preserves clarity.
7. If `context` and `passages` conflict, prefer facts that are explicitly present in the `passages`: update the concise answer to match passage-supported content rather than preserving a contradicted `context`.
8. If the retrieved `passages` do not supply explicit supporting evidence for named entities in `context`, keep the `summary` concise and do not expand or speculate.
9. Output exactly one field named `summary` whose value is the integrated post-retrieval summary text. Do not output additional fields, metadata, or commentary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Follow these operational rules when producing `answer`:
- Use only the content of `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2` as your evidential basis. Do not introduce new facts or external information.
- If both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, set `answer` to exactly one lowercase token: yes or no. The `answer` field must contain nothing else (no punctuation, capitalization, quotes, explanation, or additional text).
- Decide which token to emit by aggregating the visible summaries only. Treat `summary_2` as the later integrated evidence; do not let unresolved language in `summary_1` alone prevent emitting the single-token result if both summaries now unambiguously agree.
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, incomplete, hedged such that the relation is unresolved, or if the question is non-binary, do not apply the single-token rule; instead follow the agent's existing final-answer fallback behavior for unresolved or non-binary cases (which may produce a fuller response or an uncertainty signal).

Produce only the `answer` field as specified.

This was the second year the conference used the round robin tournament setup.  The winners of each group at the end of the round robin faced each other in a one-game match for the championship.

Big 12 Conference Baseball Tournament | The Big 12 Conference Baseball Tournament (sometimes known simply as the Big 12 Tournament) is the conference championship tournament in baseball for the Big 12 Conference.  Going back to the original format abandoned in 2005, the tournament consists of two separate four-team double-elimination tournaments.  The winners of each of those tournament face each other in a one-game match for the championship.  The winner receives the conference's automatic bid to the NCAA Division I Baseball Tournament.

response.summary_1:
- Fact: [1] "'Texas A&M won the tournament and earned the Big 12 Conference's automatic bid'" (identifies the 2007 tournament winner).
- Missing/bridge entity: Texas A&M (the 2007 winner) — passages do not state Texas A&M's conference affiliation prior to 1996 (missing fact).
- Ambiguity note: "Texas A&M" in passages is unambiguous (only one candidate present).
- Next-hop retrieval targets (prioritized):
  1. Fetch the "Texas A&M Aggies" (or "Texas A&M University athletics") page — extract the "Conference affiliations", "History", or "Conference membership" section and the years listing (look for conference membership immediately before 1996).
  2. Fetch the "Texas A&M Aggies baseball" page — extract any "Conference affiliations" or "History" section confirming the baseball team's conference before 1996.
  3. Fetch the "Southwest Conference" page — extract membership list or "Former members" with years to confirm Texas A&M's membership dates.
- Note: No authoritative Texas A&M or Southwest Conference page is present in the provided passages; retrieve those to answer conclusively.
hop2_docs: 1996 Southwest Conference Baseball Tournament | The 1996 Southwest Conference Baseball Tournament was the league's annual postseason tournament used to determine the Southwest Conference's (SWC) automatic bid to the 1996 NCAA Division I Baseball Tournament.  The tournament was held from May 15 through May 18 at Dan Law Field on the campus of Texas Tech University in Lubbock, Texas and drew a total attendance of 31,542.  This was the first time the SWC Tournament was held in Lubbock, and the final conference tournament before the conference disbanded.

Texas Conference | The Texas Conference was a NAIA/NCAA-affiliated college athletic conference in the United States that existed from 1926 to 1956.  It was composed mostly of schools from Texas, but also one from New Mexico.

1996 Kansas Jayhawks football team | The 1996 Kansas Jayhawks football team represented the University of Kansas in the 1996 NCAA Division I-A football season.  They were coached by head coach Glen Mason, who resigned after the season to become the head coach at Minnesota and played their home games at Memorial Stadium in Lawrence, Kansas.  It was the Jayhawks first year in the newly formed Big 12 Conference.  The Big 12 conference was formed by the eight teams of the recently dissolved Big Eight Conference and was joined by Baylor, Texas, Texas A&M, and Texas Tech, all formally of the Southwest Conference which had dissolved following the 1995–1996 school year as well.

1996–97 Iowa State Cyclones men's basketball team | The 1996–97 Iowa State Cyclones men's basketball team represented Iowa State University during the 1996–97 NCAA Division I men's basketball season.  The Cyclones were coached by Tim Floyd, who was in his 3rd season.  They played their home games at Hilton Coliseum in Ames, Iowa and is an original member of the newly created Big 12 Conference.  The Big 12 conference was formed by the eight teams of the recently dissolved Big Eight Conference and was joined by Baylor, Texas, Texas A&M, and Texas Tech, all formally of the Southwest Conference which had also dissolved following the 1995–1996 school year.

Baylor–Texas Tech football rivalry | The Baylor–Texas Tech football rivalry is an American college football rivalry between the Baylor Bears and Texas Tech Red Raiders.  Each school is a member of the Big 12 Conference.  The rivalry began in 1929.  The game has been played every year since 1956 despite the fact that Texas Tech was a member of the Border Intercollegiate Athletic Conference.  In 1960, Texas Tech joined the Southwest Conference, ensuring the rivalry would continue.  In 1996, the Southwest Conference dissolved, and both teams were invited, along with the Texas Longhorns and Texas A&M Aggies, with former members of the Big Eight Conference to form the Big 12 Conference.  From 1947–64, Baylor won 14 of the 15 games.  From 1996–2010, Texas Tech won 15 straight games.  In the most recent meeting of the two teams, Texas Tech snapped Baylor's 5 game win streak to bring the series record to a tie.

Heartland Conference | The Heartland Conference is a collegiate athletic conference affiliated with the National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA)'s Division II level, which was founded in 1999.  The majority of members are in Texas, with additional members in Arkansas, Kansas, and Oklahoma.  The conference office is located in Waco, Texas.

1996–97 Kansas Jayhawks men's basketball team | The 1996–97 Kansas Jayhawks men's basketball team represented the University of Kansas in the 1996-97 NCAA Division I men's basketball season, which was the Jayhawks' 99th basketball season.  The head coach was Roy Williams, who served his 9th year at KU.  The team played its home games in Allen Fieldhouse in Lawrence, Kansas.  It was the Jayhawks first year in the newly formed Big 12 Conference.  The Big 12 conference was formed by the eight teams of the recently dissolved Big Eight Conference and was joined by Baylor, Texas, Texas A&M, and Texas Tech, all formally of the Southwest Conference which had dissolved following the 1995–1996 school year as well.
hop2_titles: 1996–97 Iowa State Cyclones men's basketball team
response.summary_2: Texas A&M won the tournament and earned the Big 12 Conference's automatic bid. Texas A&M was a member of the Southwest Conference, which dissolved following the 1995–96 school year.
